articles.title: AI Broke Vulnerability Management. That's Why CISOs Are Moving Budget to BAS.

It changed the other side of the

keypoints: AI accelerated exploit development, eliminating vulnerability patching buffer

ai_summary.action_today: Enable passwordless sign-in on your primary email account today — Settings → Security → Passkeys

articles.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
articles.summary: A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.

keypoints: The Gentlemen gang ranks second in active ransomware operations
90 percent ransom split attracts skilled hackers rapidly

analysis.title: Aggressive Recruitment Strategy
analysis.desc: The Gentlemen offers unprecedented 90% ransom cuts to affiliates, outcompeting other ransomware groups for talent acquisition.

analysis.title: Rapid Operational Growth
analysis.desc: Second-place victim count ranking demonstrates swift market penetration and scaling among active ransomware-as-a-service operations.

analysis.title: Leadership Identity Investigation
analysis.desc: Security researchers examining administrative clues to unmask real-world identity behind The Gentlemen ransomware operation.

ai_summary.suggested_title: Ransomware Gang 'The Gentlemen' Revealed—90% Split Drives Growth
ai_summary.bottom_line: Your company could already be in their target pipeline—they're recruiting faster than law enforcement responds.
ai_summary.action_today: Check your email security logs for suspicious admin access attempts from last 30 days

articles.title: Microsoft patches Exchange Server zero-day exploited in attacks
articles.summary: Microsoft has patched an actively exploited Exchange Server vulnerability that allows threat actors to execute arbitrary JavaScript code in cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks targeting Outlook Web Access users. [...]

keypoints: Exchange Server zero-day actively exploited in attacks
XSS vulnerability enables arbitrary JavaScript execution in Outlook Web Access

analysis.title: Attack Vector and Scope
analysis.desc: Threat actors exploit XSS flaw in Exchange Server to inject malicious JavaScript, targeting Outlook Web Access users with direct browser-based attacks.

analysis.title: Microsoft's Response
analysis.desc: Microsoft released emergency patch to address actively exploited zero-day vulnerability, closing attack surface before widespread compromise occurs.

analysis.title: Organizational Risk
analysis.desc: On-premises Exchange deployments remain vulnerable until patched. Immediate action required to prevent credential theft and account takeover via XSS injection.

ai_summary.suggested_title: Exchange Server Zero-Day Patched — Update Immediately
ai_summary.bottom_line: Unpatched Exchange servers are actively compromised right now. Your users' credentials are being stolen.
ai_summary.action_today: Microsoft Update → Check Exchange Server version → Download and install latest patch immediately

articles.title: Your Automated Pentest Looks Clean. See What It Missed in This Expert Webinar
articles.summary: Your pentest report looks clean. That might be the problem. Run automated pentesting long enough, and the new findings start to dry up. By the third or fourth run, fewer issues appear. The report looks stable. Leadership reads "stable" as "secure." It usually isn't. The work slows down. The risk does not. That gap is what a The Hacker News webinar with Picus Security sets out to close. Autumn

keypoints: Automated pentests plateau after repeated cycles
False security from stable reports masks actual risk

analysis.title: Automation's diminishing returns
analysis.desc: Repeated automated scans find fewer issues over time, creating false confidence in security posture.

analysis.title: Leadership misinterprets stability
analysis.desc: Executives equate flat pentest reports with actual security, overlooking gaps automation cannot detect.

analysis.title: Expert-led testing required
analysis.desc: Webinar reveals findings automated tools miss, bridging gap between perceived and actual security.

ai_summary.suggested_title: Automated Pentests Miss Critical Vulnerabilities — Expert Review Needed
ai_summary.bottom_line: Your clean pentest report probably means your tools stopped looking, not that you're safe.
ai_summary.action_today: Schedule manual penetration test with external security firm this quarter — don't rely on automation alone

articles.title: Microsoft Patches Record 206 Flaws, Including Three Zero-Days and Critical RCE Bugs
articles.summary: Microsoft on Tuesday released fixes for a record 206 security vulnerabilities impacting its software portfolio, including three flaws that have been publicly disclosed at the time of release. Of the 206 flaws, 39 are rated Critical, and 167 are rated Important in severity. This includes 63 privilege escalation, 56 remote code execution, 30 information disclosure, 27 spoofing, 20 security

keypoints: Microsoft releases 206 patches including three zero-days
56 remote code execution flaws demand immediate action

analysis.title: Scale unprecedented in patch history
analysis.desc: 206 vulnerabilities with 39 critical severity ratings represent largest monthly release, indicating widespread exposure across Microsoft ecosystem.

analysis.title: Zero-day public disclosure heightens risk
analysis.desc: Three already-disclosed flaws give attackers ready-made exploitation methods without patch lag, forcing urgent prioritization.

analysis.title: Code execution dominates threat landscape
analysis.desc: 56 RCE vulnerabilities enable complete system compromise; 63 privilege escalation flaws compound attack chain severity.

ai_summary.suggested_title: Critical Microsoft Patches 206 Flaws — Zero-Days Already Public
ai_summary.bottom_line: Three zero-days are already in attackers' hands. Your Windows box is next unless patched today.
ai_summary.action_today: Windows → Settings → System → About → Check for updates (install immediately)
